text: After entering the thoracic cavity, the abnormal artery arising from the descending aorta was identified 1 cm just above the diaphragm.
The artery was dissected and was transected using NDGIA vascular stapler.
Then we opened the inferior pulmonary ligament and we dissected the inferior pulmonary vein.
Then we completed the fissure and we dissected the arterial trunk and the bronchus of the left lower loop.
Here is the stump of the abnormal artery.
And here is the stump of the inferior pulmonary vein.
We took out the loop using endobag.
We inserted regular chest tube 16 French throughout the same surgical incision.
Then lung inflation under vision by pulling out the endotracheal tube to the level of carina.
